items.front: IFR vertical separation: up to FL 410
items.back: 1,000 feet.

items.front: IFR vertical separation: FL 290+ non-RVSM vs all
items.back: 2,000 feet.
2,000 feet.

items.front: IFR vertical separation: oceanic >FL 450 supersonic vs any
items.back: 4,000 feet.

items.front: IFR vertical separation: above FL 600 military vs military
items.back: 5,000 feet.
Odd cardinal altitudes at 2,000-foot intervals (3,000, 5,000, FL 310, FL 330...).
Even cardinal altitudes at 2,000-foot intervals (4,000, 6,000, FL 320, FL 340...).
Odd cardinal flight levels at 4,000-foot intervals beginning with FL 450.
Odd cardinal flight levels at 4,000-foot intervals beginning with FL 430.
Any cardinal altitude or flight level.
Any altitude or flight level.
Altitude blocks as requested. Any altitude or flight level.

items.front: Non-standard altitude: what can be assigned (4-5-3)
items.back: Any cardinal altitude/FL below FL 410, or any odd cardinal FL at/above FL 410, without regard to direction.

items.front: Non-standard altitude for traffic: within-facility condition
items.back: Prior approval from affected positions/sectors, or covered in a Facility Directive.

items.front: Non-standard altitude for traffic: beyond-facility condition
items.back: LOA between appropriate facilities.

items.front: Non-standard altitude for aircraft limitations: condition
items.back: Pilot informs appropriate altitude exceeds aircraft limits + prior approval from affected positions/sectors.
None.
500 feet.
1,000 feet.
1,500 feet.
2,000 feet.

items.front: Below MEA but not below MOCA: three conditions
items.back: At least 300 ft above controlled airspace floor AND (1) within 22 NM of VOR/VORTAC/TACAN, or (2) radar with MVA/MIA + lost comm instructions, or (3) GNSS equipped.

items.front: No MCA specified: when to climb to higher MEA
items.back: Prior to or immediately after passing the fix where the higher MEA is designated.
Prior to the fix so as to cross at or above the MCA.
Radar service must be provided (not below prescribed minimum IFR altitude).

items.front: GNSS MEA default on high altitude ATS routes
items.back: FL 180 unless published higher.

items.front: Direct to fix after climb/descend via clearance
items.back: Must restate 'climb via' or 'descend via.' SID/STAR name need not be restated.

items.front: Deviation from climb/descend via: controller action
items.back: State altitude to maintain and advise if intending to resume at a waypoint/fix.

items.front: Descend via: when NOT to use
items.back: When procedures contain only published 'expect' altitude and/or speed restrictions.
Cancels altitude restrictions (not speed) unless restated or modified.

items.front: Climb via SID except after (fix) maintain (altitude)
items.back: Comply with restrictions until fix, then maintain altitude until ATC clears further.

items.front: Descend via with revised crossing altitude phraseology
items.back: DESCEND VIA (STAR) ARRIVAL, EXCEPT CROSS (fix), (revised altitude).
